entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop species Sorghum bicolor (L.) Moench studied in the experiment

entities.name: RS 610
entities.description: A sorghum hybrid analyzed for growth and yield

entities.name: FEMALE PARENT
entities.description: The female parent of the sorghum hybrid RS 610

entities.name: MALE PARENT
entities.description: The male parent of the sorghum hybrid RS 610

entities.name: GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate at which the plant grows

entities.name: LEAF AREA INDEX
entities.description: The ratio of upper leaf surface area to ground area

entities.name: NET ASSIMILATION RATE
entities.description: The rate of increase in dry weight per unit leaf area

entities.name: DRY MATTER DISTRIBUTION
entities.description: The allocation of dry matter among aboveground plant parts

entities.name: DRY WEIGHT
entities.description: The mass of the plant material after water removal

entities.name: LEAF AREA
entities.description: The total surface area of the leaves

entities.name: CROP GROWTH RATE
entities.description: The rate of dry matter accumulation per unit area and time

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of grain produced by the plant

entities.name: KERNELS PER PANICLE
entities.description: The number of kernels produced per panicle

entities.name: SEEDLING GROWTH
entities.description: The growth rate and development during the seedling stage

entities.name: GRAIN FILLING PERIOD
entities.description: The duration of the grain filling stage

entities.name: REPLICATED FIELD EXPERIMENT
entities.description: The experimental design used to determine growth parameters

entities.name: GROWING SEASON
entities.description: The period from planting to harvest

entities.name: FLOWERING
entities.description: The stage of the plant's life cycle when flowers develop

entities.name: PANICLE INITIATION
entities.description: The stage when the panicle begins to form

entities.name: GRAIN FILLING
entities.description: The physiological stage where dry matter is converted to grain

relationships.description: exhibited higher from emergence to panicle initiation
superiority attributed to high CGR of female parent
superiority attributed to effective conversion of dry matter to grain of male parent
